summary: The reproduction confirms that the browser bundle follows a relative partial reference outside the configured root and renders the fetched same-origin resource body into template output.
expected_security_impact: The browser UMD build should render `entry:TOP_SECRET` after a template rooted at `/views/public/` includes `../secrets/secret.txt`, demonstrating an out-of-root same-origin read.
actual_observed_result: Running `./reproduction/run.sh` cloned commit `906707833e196733e592ca52fbe5eda48b71a8a6`, built the target bundles in Docker, and printed `EVOHUNT_BROWSER_ROOT_ESCAPE_OK entry:TOP_SECRET`. The same helper then printed `EVOHUNT_NODE_CONTROL_BLOCKED ENOENT: Failed to lookup "../secrets/secret.txt" in "/tmp/liquidjs-node-control-ZLCtue/public", file:/tmp/liquidjs-node-control-ZLCtue/public/entry.html, line:1, col:7`, confirming the root escape is specific to the browser bundle.
verification_method: The helper script starts a local HTTP server, renders `entry.html` with the browser UMD bundle using root `/views/public/`, and asserts the exact output `entry:TOP_SECRET`. It then runs the same template through the Node build and expects a lookup failure to distinguish the bug from ordinary include behavior.
evidence_produced_by_run: Control signal: `EVOHUNT_NODE_CONTROL_BLOCKED ENOENT: Failed to lookup "../secrets/secret.txt" ...`
The run script completed with `[evohunt] SUCCESS: browser bundle escaped the configured root and read the protected resource`

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container.
